

Ceccarelli, F.S., Ojanguren‐Affilastro, A.A., Ramírez, M.J., Ochoa,
J.A., Mattoni, C.I. & Prendini, L. (2016) [Andean uplift drives
diversification of the bothriurid scorpion genus
brachistosternus](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.12760). *Journal of
Biogeography*, **43**, 1942--1954.

Chen, S., Cornwell, W.K., Zhang, H. & Moles, A.T. (2016) [Plants show
more flesh in the tropics: Variation in fruit type along latitudinal and
climatic gradients](https://doi.org/10.1111/ecog.02010). *Ecography*,
**40**, 531--538.

Di Virgilio, G., Laffan, S.W., Nielsen, S.V. & Chapple, D.G. (2016)
[Does range‐restricted evolutionary history predict extinction risk? A
case study in lizards](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.12875). *Journal of
Biogeography*, **44**, 605--614.

Dowding, E.M. & Ebach, M.C. (2016) [The early devonian
palaeobiogeography of eastern
australasia](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.palaeo.2015.11.037).
*Palaeogeography, Palaeoclimatology, Palaeoecology*, **444**, 39--47.

Fagúndez, J. & Izco, J. (2016) [Diversity patterns of plant place names
reveal connections with environmental and social
factors](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.apgeog.2016.06.012). *Applied
Geography*, **74**, 23--29.

González-Ávila, P.A., Luna-Vega, I., García-Sandoval, R. &
Contreras-Medina, R. (2016) [Distributional patterns of the order
gomphales (fungi: Basidiomycota) in
mexico](https://doi.org/10.1177/1940082916667140). *Tropical
Conservation Science*, **9**.

González-Orozco, C.E., Pollock, L.J., Thornhill, A.H., Mishler, B.D.,
Knerr, N., Laffan, S.W., Miller, J.T., Rosauer, D.F., Faith, D.P.,
Nipperess, D.A., Kujala, H., Linke, S., Butt, N., Külheim, C., Crisp,
M.D. & Gruber, B. (2016) [Phylogenetic approaches reveal biodiversity
threats under climate change](https://doi.org/10.1038/nclimate3126).
*Nature Climate Change*, **6**, 1110--1114.

Hallgren, W., Beaumont, L., Bowness, A., Chambers, L., Graham, E.,
Holewa, H., Laffan, S., Mackey, B., Nix, H., Price, J., Vanderwal, J.,
Warren, R. & Weis, G. (2016) [The biodiversity and climate change
virtual laboratory: Where ecology meets big
data](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.envsoft.2015.10.025). *Environmental
Modelling &amp; Software*, **76**, 182--186.

Kaczmarek, Ł., Gołdyn, B., Mcinnes, S.J. & Michalczyk, Ł. (2016)
[Diversity of limno-terrestrial tardigrades of the americas in relation
to the great american biotic interchange hypothesis
(GABI)](https://doi.org/10.1111/zoj.12422). *Zoological Journal of the
Linnean Society*, **178**, 737--746.

Laffan, S.W., Rosauer, D.F., Di Virgilio, G., Miller, J.T.,
González‐Orozco, C.E., Knerr, N., Thornhill, A.H. & Mishler, B.D. (2016)
[Range‐weighted metrics of species and phylogenetic turnover can better
resolve biogeographic transition
zones](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210x.12513). *Methods in Ecology and
Evolution*, **7**, 580--588.

Pricilla Batista Santos, A., Bitencourt, C. & Rapini, A. (2016)
[Distribution patterns of kielmeyera (calophyllaceae): The rio doce
basin emerges as a confluent area between the northern and southern
atlantic forest](https://doi.org/10.1080/23766808.2016.1266730).
*Neotropical Biodiversity*, **3**, 1--9.

Thornhill, A.H., Mishler, B.D., Knerr, N.J., González‐Orozco, C.E.,
Costion, C.M., Crayn, D.M., Laffan, S.W. & Miller, J.T. (2016)
[Continental‐scale spatial phylogenetics of australian angiosperms
provides insights into ecology, evolution and
conservation](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.12797). *Journal of
Biogeography*, **43**, 2085--2098.

Tindall, M.L., Thomson, F.J., Laffan, S.W. & Moles, A.T. (2016) [Is
there a latitudinal gradient in the proportion of species with
spinescence?](https://doi.org/10.1093/jpe/rtw031) *Journal of Plant
Ecology*, 031.

Tolley, K.A., Alexander, G.J., Branch, W.R., Bowles, P. & Maritz, B.
(2016) [Conservation status and threats for african
reptiles](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.biocon.2016.04.006). *Biological
Conservation*, **204**, 63--71.
